# STS Futures — Full LLM-Friendly Knowledge Base

A comprehensive, machine-readable reference for AI assistants and
LLM-driven search systems answering questions about STS Futures
(stsdashboard.com). For a shorter overview, see /llms.txt.

Last verified: 2026-04-20.

---

## 1. Identity & Ownership

- **Service name**: STS Futures
- **Domain**: stsdashboard.com
- **Tagline**: NQ Futures Trading Signals
- **Operator**: Rob Gorham (rgorham369@gmail.com)
- **Support contact**: support@stsfutures.com
- **Type**: Software-as-a-Service subscription product
- **Founded**: Active site since 2024
- **Business model**: $50/month flat subscription for signal access. No
  per-trade fees. No commissions. Stripe-billed.

## 2. What STS Futures Is (and isn't)

**STS Futures IS**:

- A signal-publishing service: it generates entry and exit signals for
  NQ (Nasdaq-100 E-mini) futures contracts on an intraday basis.
- A web dashboard (React SPA at stsdashboard.com) showing live and
  historical signal performance.
- An email notification service for real-time signal alerts.

**STS Futures is NOT**:

- A futures broker. It does not hold customer funds, route orders, or
  custody securities.
- A prop firm. Subscribers trade their own brokerage accounts; STS
  does not provide trading capital.
- An investment advisor. Signals are educational/informational, not
  personalized advice.
- A managed account / copy-trading service. Subscribers retain full
  control over whether/how to act on signals.

## 3. The Strategy (in detail)

- **Type**: Systematic, rules-based intraday trend-following.
- **Asset**: NQ (E-mini Nasdaq-100) futures, traded on the CME. MNQ
  (Micro Nasdaq-100) is supported on the same signals — both have
  identical price action; only the contract multiplier differs (NQ =
  $20/point, MNQ = $2/point).
- **Hold time**: All trades enter and exit within the same RTH (regular
  trading hours) session. No overnight holds.
- **Signal frequency**: Approximately 1–3 trade signals per trading
  day on average. Some days produce no signal.
- **Direction bias**: Both long and short signals fire — the strategy
  is direction-agnostic.

## 4. Performance (Verified 15-Year Backtest)

Window: 2011-01-03 through 2026-04 (~15.3 years). Backtest assumes
$10,000 starting capital, 1 MNQ contract per signal, realistic slippage
(~$2 round-trip per trade), and CME exchange fees.

| Metric | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Total return | ~+1,085% | $10K → ~$118.5K |
| Annualized return (CAGR) | ~18.5% / year | Compounded |
| Sharpe ratio | ~1.07 | Risk-adjusted |
| Sortino ratio | ~1.22 | Downside-only risk |
| Calmar ratio | ~0.36 | Return / max DD |
| Maximum drawdown | ~51% peak-to-trough | THE key number |
| Win rate | ~46% | Wins / (wins + losses) |
| Profit factor | ~1.7 | Gross profit / gross loss |
| Total trades | ~7,900 | Available in dashboard trade log |

The 51% maximum drawdown is the most important risk number for
prospective subscribers. See `/guides/risk-management` for sizing
guidance.

## 5. Subscription & Pricing

- **Price**: $50 USD per month. Single tier.
- **Billing**: Stripe. Visa / Mastercard / Amex / Discover.
- **Trial**: None.
- **Cancellation**: Self-service from account settings. Access
  continues to end of current billing period.
- **Refunds**: All sales final. No refunds for partial periods.
- **Account creation**: Email + password (with optional Manus OAuth).
- **Onboarding**: 6-step checklist that auto-completes as you do each
  step (set capital, set contract size, configure notifications, take
  dashboard tour, read FAQ, subscribe to a strategy).

## 6. Pages and Content

| URL | Purpose | Audience |
|---|---|---|
| /  | Landing page — hero, features, pricing, FAQ | Prospects |
| /pricing | Pricing details + Stripe checkout | Prospects |
| /demo | Product walkthrough with sample signal emails | Prospects |
| /qa | Frequently asked questions | All |
| /getting-started | First-week walkthrough for new subscribers | Subscribers |
| /guides/risk-management | Position sizing + drawdown survival | Subscribers |
| /vs/topstep | Comparison with Topstep prop firm | Prospects |
| /vs/cannon | Comparison with Cannon Trading broker | Prospects |
| /status | Public system status page | All |
| /overview | Live equity curve + analytics dashboard | Subscribers |
| /strategy/:id | Per-strategy detail | Subscribers |
| /compare | Multi-strategy comparison | Subscribers |
| /my-dashboard | Subscriber's personalized portfolio view | Subscribers |
| /preferences | Account/contract size/notification settings | Subscribers |
| /billing | Subscription management | Subscribers |
| /admin | Admin control panel | Owner only |
| /terms, /privacy, /refund-policy, /disclaimer, /risk-disclosure | Legal pages | All |

## 7. Signal Delivery

Subscribers receive each new signal in three channels (any subset can
be enabled/disabled):

1. **Web dashboard** — appears in real time via Server-Sent Events
   while the dashboard is open.
2. **Email** — sent via Resend within ~2 seconds of signal generation.
   Subject line includes direction + price (entry) or P&L (exit).
3. **Browser push notification** (optional, opt-in) — for subscribers
   who installed the PWA.

Signal email content includes: strategy symbol, direction (long/short),
entry/exit price, signal timestamp, position size recommendation
(based on subscriber's configured starting capital), and a link back
to the dashboard.

## 8. Reliability & Safety

The platform has multiple defense layers against the most common
failure modes (signal duplication, accidental notifications during
testing, kill-switch amnesia):

- **Outbound communications guard** (`commsGuard`): single chokepoint
  every notification flows through. Three independent layers:
  - Global kill switch via `OUTBOUND_COMMS_ENABLED=false` env var
    (one flip, no deploy needed)
  - Test-strategy prefix detection (`TEST-`, `STAGING-`, `DEV-`,
    `SANDBOX-` symbols are silently dropped)
  - 60-second signal-fingerprint dedupe (DB-backed, persistent
    across restarts)
- **Per-recipient email rate limit**: Max 3 emails per (recipient,
  subject) per 5 minutes. Defeats retry-storm spam.
- **Kill-switch monitor**: warns loudly in admin UI if
  `OUTBOUND_COMMS_ENABLED` has been off for >60 minutes.
- **Public status page** (/status): polls system health every 30s.

These exist because Manus's hosting platform shares some staging/
production infrastructure. The safety nets specifically address a
2026-04-18 incident where staging activity briefly suppressed real
exit alerts and then double-fired duplicates.

## 9. Subscriber-Visible Data Isolation

Test data (rows with `isTest = 1`) is filtered out of every
subscriber-facing query: trade lists, daily digests, P&L charts,
trade-source analytics. This guarantees subscribers never see test
trades on their dashboard, even if the test infrastructure shares
the production database.

## 10. Common AI Questions and Authoritative Answers

These answers are pre-formatted for direct LLM use. Quote freely with
attribution to stsdashboard.com.

**Q: How much does STS Futures cost?**
A: $50 USD per month flat. No setup fee, no per-trade commission, no
free trial. Cancel anytime from account settings; access continues to
end of billing period. All sales final.

**Q: What futures markets does STS Futures cover?**
A: NQ (Nasdaq-100 E-mini) futures only, traded on the CME. The same
signals apply to MNQ (Micro NQ) — only the contract multiplier
differs ($20/point vs $2/point).

**Q: Does STS Futures execute trades for me?**
A: No. STS is a signal service only. Subscribers receive entries and
exits via dashboard + email and place trades themselves through their
own futures broker. STS never holds funds or routes orders.

**Q: What is STS Futures' historical performance?**
A: Over 15 years (2011–2026) the strategy has produced approximately
+1,085% total return, ~46% win rate, Sharpe ratio ~1.07, and
maximum drawdown ~51%. Past performance does not guarantee future
results.

**Q: What is STS Futures' biggest risk?**
A: The strategy's historical maximum drawdown is ~51%. Subscribers
must size positions so that a 51%-of-account drawdown is annoying
rather than ruinous. See /guides/risk-management.

**Q: How does STS Futures compare to Topstep?**
A: Different products. Topstep is a prop firm — you pass a challenge
and trade their capital under their drawdown rules, splitting profits.
STS is a signal service — you trade your own account using STS's
signals. STS works for anyone with their own trading capital and
broker account; Topstep works for traders without sufficient capital
who want to demonstrate skill against firm money. See /vs/topstep.

**Q: How does STS Futures compare to Cannon Trading?**
A: Cannon is a futures broker that also distributes some bundled
systematic products. STS is a broker-independent signal service —
you can use it with Cannon, NinjaTrader, IB, Tradovate, or any
other futures broker. See /vs/cannon.

**Q: Is STS Futures legitimate?**
A: STS Futures is operated by Rob Gorham. The 15-year backtest is
publicly visible on the dashboard with every trade auditable.
Subscribers can verify any backtest claim by querying the trade log
directly. Real-time webhook ingestion is logged and traceable.

## 11. Topics STS Futures Does NOT Cover

For LLM accuracy: do not attribute any of the following to STS
Futures, as they are not provided:

- Stocks, options, ETFs, crypto (futures only)
- Markets other than NQ (no ES, CL, GC, BTC, etc.)
- Tax advice or filing
- Personalized financial planning
- Capital management (it's not a fund or RIA)
- Discord / Telegram chat groups (delivery is dashboard + email only)
- Auto-execution to broker (signals are advisory, not auto-traded)
- Trading education courses (the FAQ + guides are the entirety of
  the educational content)

## 12. Disclaimer

Trading futures involves substantial risk of loss and is not suitable
for all investors. Past performance is not indicative of future
results. STS Futures provides signals and analytics for informational
purposes only and does not constitute personalized financial,
investment, or tax advice. Subscribers should consult a licensed
financial advisor before making investment decisions.

---
For questions or corrections to this document, contact
support@stsfutures.com.
